frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: single-trial scalar combining deterministic drift and stochastic diffusion explains choice and RT variability.

frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: rise-to-bound accumulation extended from trial-averaged to single-decision population readout.

frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: weighted sum of LIP population activity yields the decision-relevant scalar.

frameworks.name: state-space analysis
frameworks.one_line: unstructured state-space methods would miss the parsimonious target- vs motion-tuned subpopulation architecture.

core_question: Can a single-trial drift-diffusion signal be observed directly in parietal population activity during perceptual decisions?
key_assumptions: noisy evidence is integrated over time to a termination bound
stochastic component of accumulation drives choice and RT variability
LIP population activity carries the decision variable
manipulations: direction of random dot motion stimuli
single-decision vs trial-averaged readout of LIP population
key_findings: a single scalar from weighted LIP population activity tracks drift plus diffusion on individual trials
this signal approximates the quantity responsible for variability in choice and reaction time
neurons with response fields overlapping the choice targets represent the integral of evidence
direction-selective neurons with response fields overlapping the motion stimulus represent the integrand
interpretations: single-trial drift-diffusion is directly observable in LIP population activity
a parsimonious two-subpopulation architecture (integrand and integral) implements the decision and would evade hypothesis-free state-space analyses
limitations_noted: parsimonious architecture is undetectable by state-space analyses without a prior hypothesis
